close_reason: Object handle caching would require invalidation on token removal/reinsertion. Per-operation lookup is safer for HSM security.
description:
Trigger: sign() is called repeatedly on the same backend.

Behavior: every sign() calls find_object(session, label, ObjectClass::PRIVATE_KEY) which does a C_FindObjectsInit + C_FindObjects + C_FindObjectsFinal triplet (3 PKCS#11 round-trips). For network-backed HSMs this is real latency. For local SoftHSM2 it's ~50µs per call.

Note: object handles are valid only within a session. If sessions are also cached (see crate-secretsx-nde2.16), handles can be cached alongside. If sessions are not cached, this finding is moot — handles must be re-found per session anyway.

Defer until the session-caching design (.16) is decided. Mark as blocked-by .16.
title: Object handles re-resolved on every sign(); could be cached after first detect_algorithm
